tagline: Find your federal income tax bracket and estimated tax
keywords: tax bracket, income tax, federal tax, marginal rate
calcFn: var rate = status === 'Single' ? (income > 191950 ? 32 : income > 100525 ? 24 : income > 47150 ? 22 : income > 11600 ? 12 : 10) : (income > 383900 ? 32 : income > 201050 ? 24 : income > 94300 ? 22 : income > 23200 ? 12 : 10); var tax = income * rate / 100 * 0.75; var effective = (tax / income) * 100; return {bracket: fmt(rate,0), tax: dollar(tax), effective: fmt(effective,1)};

faq.q: What is my tax bracket?
faq.a: The rate applied to your last dollar of income.

faq.q: Marginal vs effective?
faq.a: Effective rate is always lower since earlier income is taxed at lower rates.

formulas.name: Tax
formulas.explanation: Each portion of income taxed at its bracket rate.

tagline: Calculate monthly payments for any loan
keywords: loan payment, monthly payment, loan calculator, interest
calcFn: var r = rate/100/12; var payment = amount * r / (1 - Math.pow(1+r,-months)); var total = payment * months; var totalInterest = total - amount; return {payment: dollar(payment), totalInterest: dollar(totalInterest), total: dollar(total)};

faq.q: How do I lower my payment?
faq.a: Longer term, lower rate, or smaller loan amount.

faq.q: Fixed vs variable rate?
faq.a: Fixed stays the same. Variable can change, usually starting lower.

tagline: Calculate soil or fill dirt needed for any project
keywords: soil, fill dirt, topsoil, garden soil, volume
calcFn: var cuFt = length * width * (depth/12); var cuYd = cuFt / 27; var tons = cuYd * 1.1; var cost = cuYd * 30; return {cuYd: fmt(cuYd,2), tons: fmt(tons,1), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: How much does soil weigh?
faq.a: About 1.1 tons per cubic yard for topsoil.

faq.q: How deep for a garden?
faq.a: 6-8 inches for most gardens, 12+ for raised beds.

tagline: Calculate the right AC size for your room
keywords: AC BTU, air conditioner, cooling, room size, BTU calculator
calcFn: var baseBtu = sqft * 20; var sunMod = {'Heavily Shaded': 0.9, 'Average': 1, 'Very Sunny': 1.1}; var btu = baseBtu * (sunMod[sunExposure] || 1) + (occupants - 2) * 600; btu = Math.ceil(btu / 1000) * 1000; var tonnage = btu / 12000; var estCost = (btu / 12000) * 0.15 * 8 * 30; return {btu: fmt(btu,0), tonnage: fmt(tonnage,2), estCost: dollar(estCost)};

faq.q: How many BTU per square foot?
faq.a: About 20 BTU per sq ft as a baseline, adjusted for sun, occupants, and insulation.

faq.q: What is a ton of cooling?
faq.a: 12,000 BTU/hour. A typical room AC is 0.5-1.5 tons.

formulas.name: BTU
formulas.explanation: Base calculation with sun and occupancy adjustments.

tagline: Calculate wallpaper rolls needed for your room
keywords: wallpaper, wallpaper rolls, wall covering, wallpaper calculator
calcFn: var wallArea = 2 * (length + width) * height; wallArea -= doors * 21 + windows * 15; var usableSqFt = 25; var rolls = Math.ceil(wallArea / usableSqFt); var cost = rolls * 40; return {wallArea: fmt(wallArea,0), rolls: fmt(rolls,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: How much does a roll cover?
faq.a: A standard single roll covers about 28-30 sq ft, minus waste about 25 usable sq ft.

faq.q: Should I order extra?
faq.a: Yes, order 10-15% extra for pattern matching and mistakes.

formulas.name: Rolls
formulas.formula: Rolls = (Wall Area - Openings) / 25 sq ft per roll
formulas.explanation: Total wall area minus doors/windows, divided by usable roll coverage.

tagline: Calculate your estimated due date from last menstrual period
keywords: due date, pregnancy calculator, EDD, gestational age
calcFn: var lmp = new Date(lmpYear, lmpMonth-1, lmpDay); var due = new Date(lmp.getTime() + 280*24*60*60*1000); var now = new Date(); var daysDiff = Math.floor((now - lmp) / (1000*60*60*24)); var weeksNow = Math.floor(daysDiff / 7); var dueStr = (due.getMonth()+1) + '/' + due.getDate() + '/' + due.getFullYear(); var trimester = weeksNow < 13 ? 'First' : weeksNow < 27 ? 'Second' : 'Third'; if (weeksNow < 0 || weeksNow > 45) { weeksNow = 0; trimester = 'Check dates'; } return {dueDate: dueStr, weeksNow: fmt(weeksNow,0), trimester: trimester};

faq.q: How is the due date calculated?
faq.a: Add 280 days (40 weeks) to the first day of your last menstrual period.

faq.q: How accurate is the due date?
faq.a: Only about 5% of babies arrive on their due date. Most come within 2 weeks.

formulas.name: Naegele's Rule
formulas.explanation: Standard calculation adding 40 weeks to last menstrual period.

tagline: Calculate concrete needed for poured steps
keywords: concrete steps, stairs, porch steps, concrete volume
calcFn: var totalCuFt = 0; for (var i = 1; i <= numSteps; i++) { totalCuFt += width * (treadDepth/12) * (riserHeight/12 * i); } totalCuFt *= 1.1; var cuYd = totalCuFt / 27; var bags80 = Math.ceil(totalCuFt / 0.6); var cost = cuYd * 150; return {cuYd: fmt(cuYd,2), bags80: fmt(bags80,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: Standard step dimensions?
faq.a: 7-7.75 inch riser, 10-11 inch tread per building code.

faq.q: Do I need rebar in steps?
faq.a: Yes, use #4 rebar for structural integrity and crack prevention.

formulas.name: Volume
formulas.formula: Sum of each step's volume (accumulated height)
formulas.explanation: Each step includes all concrete below it.

tagline: Calculate paint mixing ratios for custom colors
keywords: paint mixing, custom color, paint ratio, color blending
calcFn: var total = color1Pct + color2Pct; var base = totalGallons * (color1Pct / total); var tint = totalGallons * (color2Pct / total); var baseOz = base * 128; var tintOz = tint * 128; return {base: fmt(base,2), tint: fmt(tint,2), baseOz: fmt(baseOz,0), tintOz: fmt(tintOz,0)};

faq.q: Can I mix different paint brands?
faq.a: Same-type paints (latex with latex) can be mixed. Avoid mixing oil and water-based.

faq.q: How to test a custom color?
faq.a: Always mix a small test batch first and let it dry completely before committing.

formulas.name: Ratio
formulas.explanation: Proportional mixing based on desired percentages.

tagline: Calculate de-icing salt needed for your driveway and walkways
keywords: ice melt, road salt, deicer, snow removal, salt calculator
calcFn: var rates = {'Light frost': 1, 'Thin ice (< 1/4 inch)': 2, 'Moderate (1/4 - 1/2 inch)': 3, 'Heavy (> 1/2 inch)': 4}; var rate = rates[iceThickness] || 2; var tempMult = temperature < 10 ? 1.5 : temperature < 20 ? 1.2 : 1; var saltLbs = (area / 100) * rate * tempMult; var bags50 = Math.ceil(saltLbs / 50); var type = temperature < 0 ? 'Calcium chloride (works to -25°F)' : temperature < 15 ? 'Magnesium chloride (works to 0°F)' : 'Rock salt (works to 15°F)'; return {saltLbs: fmt(saltLbs,0), bags50: fmt(bags50,0), type: type};

faq.q: How much salt per square foot?
faq.a: About 2-4 lbs per 100 sq ft depending on ice thickness.

faq.q: When is rock salt ineffective?
faq.a: Below 15°F. Use calcium or magnesium chloride for colder temperatures.

formulas.name: Salt Amount
formulas.explanation: Area-based application rate adjusted for temperature.

tagline: Calculate rainwater collection potential from your roof
keywords: rain barrel, rainwater harvesting, water collection, roof runoff
calcFn: var gallonsYear = roofArea * (rainfall / 12) * 7.48 * (efficiency/100); var gallonsMonth = gallonsYear / 12; var barrels = Math.ceil(gallonsMonth / 55); return {gallonsYear: fmt(gallonsYear,0), gallonsMonth: fmt(gallonsMonth,0), barrels: fmt(barrels,0)};

faq.q: How much water can I collect?
faq.a: A 1000 sq ft roof in 40 inches of rain = ~20,000 gallons per year.

faq.q: Is collected rainwater safe?
faq.a: For irrigation yes. For drinking, it needs filtration and treatment.

formulas.name: Collection
formulas.explanation: Roof area times depth of rain times gallons per cubic foot.

tagline: Convert your dog's age to human years accurately
keywords: dog age, dog years, human years, pet age
calcFn: var firstYear = 15; var secondYear = 9; var laterYears = {'Small (under 20 lbs)': 4, 'Medium (20-50 lbs)': 5, 'Large (50-90 lbs)': 6, 'Giant (90+ lbs)': 7}; var yearRate = laterYears[size] || 5; var humanAge = dogAge <= 1 ? dogAge * firstYear : dogAge <= 2 ? firstYear + (dogAge-1) * secondYear : firstYear + secondYear + (dogAge-2) * yearRate; var lifeStage = humanAge < 15 ? 'Puppy' : humanAge < 30 ? 'Young Adult' : humanAge < 55 ? 'Adult' : humanAge < 75 ? 'Senior' : 'Geriatric'; return {humanAge: fmt(humanAge,0), lifeStage: lifeStage};

faq.q: Is it really 7 dog years per human year?
faq.a: No, the first year equals 15 human years, the second 9, then 4-7 per year based on size.

faq.q: Do small dogs age slower?
faq.a: Yes, small dogs live longer and age more slowly after year 2 than large breeds.

formulas.name: Dog to Human Years
formulas.explanation: Non-linear conversion that varies by dog size.

tagline: Estimate the cost of a new concrete driveway
keywords: driveway cost, concrete driveway, driveway estimate, paving
calcFn: var sqft = length * width; var rates = {'Concrete': 12, 'Asphalt': 7, 'Pavers': 18, 'Gravel': 3}; var costPerSqFt = rates[material] || 10; var cost = sqft * costPerSqFt; return {sqft: fmt(sqft,0), cost: dollar(cost), costPerSqFt: dollar(costPerSqFt)};

faq.q: How much does a concrete driveway cost?
faq.a: $8-15 per sq ft installed. A typical 2-car driveway costs $3,000-7,000.

faq.q: How long does concrete last?
faq.a: 30+ years with proper installation and maintenance.

formulas.name: Cost
formulas.formula: Cost = Length × Width × Price per sq ft

tagline: Calculate your cryptocurrency profit or loss
keywords: crypto profit, crypto loss, bitcoin profit, trading P&L
calcFn: var invested = buyPrice * quantity; var returned = sellPrice * quantity; var profitLoss = returned - invested; var roi = (profitLoss / invested) * 100; var afterFees = profitLoss - fees; return {profitLoss: dollar(profitLoss), roi: (roi >= 0 ? '+' : '') + fmt(roi,1), afterFees: dollar(afterFees)};

faq.q: How is crypto profit calculated?
faq.a: (Sell Price - Buy Price) × Quantity - Fees = Net Profit.

faq.q: Do I owe taxes on crypto gains?
faq.a: Yes, crypto gains are taxed as capital gains. Short-term gains are taxed as income.

formulas.name: P/L
formulas.explanation: Simple profit calculation with fee deduction.

tagline: Calculate your daily protein needs based on your goals
keywords: protein intake, daily protein, muscle building, protein calculator
calcFn: var rates = {'Sedentary/General Health': 0.36, 'Weight Loss': 0.6, 'Muscle Building': 0.8, 'Endurance Athlete': 0.7}; var dailyProtein = weight * (rates[goal] || 0.5); var perMeal = dailyProtein / meals; var chickenOz = dailyProtein / 7; var sources = fmt(chickenOz,0) + ' oz chicken breast'; return {dailyProtein: fmt(dailyProtein,0), perMeal: fmt(perMeal,0), sources: sources};

faq.q: How much protein do I need?
faq.a: 0.36g/lb for sedentary, 0.6-0.8g/lb for active/muscle building.

faq.q: Can I eat too much protein?
faq.a: Healthy kidneys can handle 1g+ per pound. Spread intake across meals for best absorption.

formulas.name: Daily Protein
formulas.explanation: Weight-based protein needs adjusted for activity and goals.

tagline: Calculate your actual miles per gallon fuel economy
keywords: MPG, fuel economy, gas mileage, miles per gallon
calcFn: var mpg = miles / gallons; var costPerMile = (gasPrice / mpg) * 100; var annualCost = (12000 / mpg) * gasPrice; return {mpg: fmt(mpg,1), costPerMile: fmt(costPerMile,1), annualCost: dollar(annualCost)};

faq.q: How do I calculate MPG?
faq.a: Divide total miles driven by gallons of fuel used.

faq.q: What is good MPG?
faq.a: 30+ MPG is good for cars, 20+ for SUVs, 15+ for trucks.

formulas.name: MPG
formulas.explanation: Simple miles divided by fuel consumed.

tagline: Calculate stain or sealer needed for your deck
keywords: deck stain, wood stain, deck sealer, deck maintenance
calcFn: var floorArea = length * width; var railingArea = railing === 'Yes' ? 2 * (length + width) * 3 * 0.5 : 0; var area = floorArea + railingArea; var gallons = Math.ceil(area * coats / 250); var cost = gallons * 40; return {area: fmt(area,0), gallons: fmt(gallons,0), cost: dollar(cost)};

faq.q: How much does deck stain cover?
faq.a: Most deck stains cover 200-300 sq ft per gallon.

faq.q: How often to restain a deck?
faq.a: Every 2-3 years for transparent stain, 3-5 for solid stain.

formulas.name: Stain
formulas.explanation: Total area times coats divided by coverage rate.
